app.name: SignLearn
app.tagline: Real-time American Sign Language ↔ English in your browser.
app.description: Have a real conversation in American Sign Language and English — no app to install, no interpreter required. Your camera feed stays on your device.
landing.heading: Sign and speak. In the same window.
landing.subhead: SignLearn is a research project that recognises American Sign Language live in the browser and turns it into captions and speech — so Deaf, hard-of-hearing, and hearing people can have a real conversation without an interpreter in the room.
landing.trust.privacy: Your video never leaves your device. Only 126 hand-landmark numbers are sent to the model.
landing.trust.offline: Works in any modern browser. No app to install.
landing.trust.open: Open source, peer-reviewable, and free to deploy.
landing.who.title: Built with the communities it serves
landing.who.deaf.title: Deaf & hard-of-hearing
landing.who.deaf.body: Captions, transcripts, and TTS at the speed of conversation — designed with Deaf community input.
landing.who.learners.title: Sign language learners
landing.who.learners.body: Practice with live feedback, structured lessons, and a curriculum that matches how ASL is actually used.
landing.who.educators.title: Educators & interpreters
landing.who.educators.body: A neutral, free tool for classroom demonstration, training, and assessment.
landing.who.researchers.title: Researchers & NGOs
landing.who.researchers.body: Open data formats, exportable transcripts, on-device privacy. Deploy in studies, clinics, or community programmes.
landing.how.title: How it works
landing.how.step1.title: Your browser sees your hands
landing.how.step1.body: MediaPipe Hands extracts 21 landmark points per hand, 30 times a second — entirely in the browser. Video stays on your device.
landing.how.step2.title: Our model reads the motion
landing.how.step2.body: A TCN sequence model trained on 36 ASL classes (and growing) recognises signs in under 6 ms per inference.
landing.how.step3.title: Everyone follows along
landing.how.step3.body: Predicted signs become captions and speech in real time. Hearing partners speak back via push-to-talk. Everything is logged for review.
landing.why.title: Why this matters
landing.why.body: Roughly 70 million people worldwide use a sign language as a primary mode of communication. Professional interpreters are scarce, expensive, and rarely available on demand. SignLearn explores whether on-device ML can offer a free, immediate, privacy-respecting bridge — and whether learners can use the same engine to build fluency.
landing.why.objectives.title: Research objectives
landing.why.objectives.list: Quantify recognition accuracy in real deployment, surface failure modes through community feedback, and study whether closed-loop practice improves learner outcomes.
landing.deploy.title: Designed for deployment
landing.deploy.body: Universities · NGOs · Accessibility offices · Deaf community organisations.

practice.subhead: Pick a sign, watch the reference, then sign it back. We'll tell you when you nail it.
